title: Understanding the Problem
text: The meteor exploded at an altitude of $$23.5$$ km, and the blast wave took $$2$$ minutes $$30$$ seconds to reach the ground. We need to calculate the speed of the blast wave and compare it to the speed of sound at sea level, which is $$343$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Convert Time to Seconds
text: First, convert the time it took for the blast wave to reach the ground from minutes and seconds to just seconds.

title: Calculate the Speed of the Blast Wave
text: Use the formula speed $$=$$ $$\frac{distance}{time}$$ to calculate the speed of the blast wave. Remember to convert the altitude from kilometers to meters.

title: Calculate Speed
text: Calculate the speed of the blast wave using the formula speed $$=$$ $$\frac{distance}{time}$$, where distance is $$23.5$$ km (converted to meters) and time is $$150$$ seconds.

title: Compare with the Speed of Sound
text: Now that you have the speed of the blast wave, compare it to the speed of sound at sea level, which is $$343$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$.

title: Calculate the Percentage of the Speed of Sound
text: Calculate what percentage of the speed of sound the blast wave's speed is. Use the formula: (speed of blast $$\frac{wave}{speed}$$ of sound) * 100%.

title: Final Comparison
text: Based on your calculations, determine how the speed of the blast wave compares to the speed of sound at sea level.
